mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales signal robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of UNRATE above 5.0% and FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals a restrictive policy regime that suppresses aggregate demand and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy with rising unemployment
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls signal demand saturation and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and corporate revenue compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing 4-quarter average signals persistent demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds its trailing average, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or occur less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI inflation rate above 4.0% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL-triggered signals produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,500 thousand units indicates contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1500 is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
